picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on STL by 7.5pp — 53.0% fair against a 44.6% price. Several smaller signals stack the same way — defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp), pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.6pp), home field (~+0.5pp) — with no single one dominating. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +120 still sits inside the playable range (to +104). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

picks.narrative: IBP makes SF 56.2% fair versus a 48.2% no-vig market price — a 7.2pp gap. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +104 still sits inside the playable range (to -109). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: A 6.8pp gap: IBP has OAK at 43.8%, the market at 37.0%. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-0.9pp): J.T. Ginn 4.31 xFIP vs Tarik Skubal 3.38 xFIP. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +170 still sits inside the playable range (to +151). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

picks.narrative: The model sees COL as a 34.2% side; the market is pricing 28.5%, leaving a 6.1pp edge. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is home field (~-1.0pp): LAD's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +257 still sits inside the playable range (to +232). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: The model sees MIN as a 58.1% side; the market is pricing 51.6%, leaving a 5.5pp edge. There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment leans to the opponent. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -111 still sits inside the playable range (to -118). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.
